name: CASIN lowered blood DNA-methylation age in aged mice
summary: The CASIN aged-mouse study links systemic Cdc42 inhibition to a younger blood DNA-methylation clock alongside lifespan and cytokine changes.
statement: A four-day systemic CASIN course in aged female C57BL/6 mice was reported to produce a younger blood DNA-methylation clock alongside reduced inflammatory cytokines and extended lifespan.
quantitative_note: The source reports significant average and maximum lifespan extension and a younger blood DNA-methylation clock, but this staged finding treats the methylation result as animal biomarker support.
caveats: This is mouse evidence and does not establish human safety or efficacy.
Cdc42 inhibition may affect inflammation, blood composition, and stem-cell biology rather than directly restoring methylation patterns.
Sex, strain, dose, and safety translation remain unresolved.
